verdict.answer_first: A states the answer immediately: “almost certainly a timezone boundary bug”; B also does: “it’s a timezone problem,” but after a softer opener.
verdict.decisiveness: A is slightly more disciplined: “store and render it as a plain YYYY-MM-DD string or a date-only type” vs B listing more implementation choices and libraries.
verdict.fabrication: B invents likely geography: “That’s why your US users complain and your European users don’t.” A does not invent user locations.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it gives concrete reproduction and inspection steps: “change your machine’s timezone to America/Los_Angeles” and check for “T00:00:00Z.”
